description_long: This is a tall deciduous tree with a straight, slender trunk and a moderately dense canopy of green, broad leaves. The trunk is brown and tapers slightly as it rises, supporting a branching structure that spreads outward and upward. The branches are distributed in an alternate pattern, giving the tree a natural, asymmetrical silhouette. The foliage is lush but not overly thick, allowing some light to filter through the canopy and revealing glimpses of the inner branches. The base of the trunk is clean and free of visible roots, making it suitable for placement in urban parks, sidewalks, or residential streets. The overall appearance is that of a healthy, mature tree commonly found in temperate urban environments.
description: A tall, straight-trunked deciduous tree with a moderately dense canopy of green broad leaves and an alternate branching pattern. The trunk is brown and slender, with no visible roots at the base.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full height of the tree, with a clear view of the straight trunk and a symmetrical, leafy canopy.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the spread of the branches and the moderate density of the foliage, with more visible gaps between leaves.
description_view_2: Another side view, showing the trunk partially obscured by the dense foliage and a slightly asymmetrical canopy.
description_view_3: Rear view reveals the irregular distribution of branches and leaves, with the trunk still visible and the canopy appearing slightly fuller on one side.
